summary: Comprehensive backend (20 pytest tests) + frontend e2e (Playwright) testing of Apex Industrial Engineering app. All backend APIs (public services, contact, proposals, auth, customer, admin proposals/customers/analytics/Excel export, services CRUD) and all critical frontend flows (home, services catalog + cart, proposal submission with APX- ref, signup -> /dashboard, admin login -> /admin, admin overview charts, admin projects edit modal, export, customers, services CRUD) pass end-to-end.

issues: Document title is generic 'Emergent | Fullstack App' instead of an Apex-branded title. Update public/index.html <title> to 'Apex Industrial Engineering Solutions'.

action_items: Update <title> in /app/frontend/public/index.html to brand-appropriate text (currently 'Emergent | Fullstack App').
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: set_auth_cookie uses secure=True + samesite='none' which is correct for cross-origin httpOnly cookies in production over HTTPS, but the frontend stores access_token in localStorage and sends it via Authorization header (this duplication is intentional per spec — confirmed working).
server.py monolithic ~557 lines; acceptable for current MVP but consider splitting into routers/auth/admin/public modules as the app grows (threshold > 700 lines).
admin_customers performs N+1 queries (one proposals.find per user). For larger customer bases use a single aggregation pipeline.
Excel export streams entire result set in-memory (openpyxl BytesIO). Fine for current scale; revisit if >5000 rows.
JWT_SECRET correctly read from env (no default). Good. ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD default fallbacks exist but seed_admin correctly upgrades existing password if env changes — verified.
All MongoDB queries already exclude _id with projection {'_id': 0}. Good.
test_credentials: Admin: apex.engg146@gmail.com / Apex@2025 (seeded). Customer: dynamic test_cust_<rand>@example.com created at runtime; signup also tested.
seed_data_creation: Test customers and proposals created via API (prefixed TEST_). Several pending proposals exist in DB after run (APX-2606-* refs).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_apex_api.py (pytest). Frontend route for proposal page is /request (NOT /request-proposal). Auth uses both httpOnly cookie + Bearer token from localStorage 'apex_token'. data-testid coverage is excellent (auth-name, auth-email, auth-password, auth-phone, auth-company, auth-submit, service-add-N, nav-cart, proposal-name/email/phone/company/budget/timeline/details/submit, admin-export-excel, admin-edit-N, admin-status-filter, add-service-btn).
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration.
